channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Pope Francis resting on day 10 in hospital for lung infection that led to early stages of kidney insufficiency
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, was resting Monday morning, his 10th day at the hospital for a complex lung infection that led to the early stages of kidney insufficiency.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Friedrich Merz on course to take Germany's top job after election, vows to unite Europe
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Friedrich Merz, on course to become post-World War II Germany's 10th chancellor after the country's election, has vowed to prioritize European unity and the continent's security as it grapples with the new Trump administration and Russia's war on Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis in critical condition with early kidney failure but remains alert as prayers pour in
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Sunday and blood tests showed early kidney failure but he remains alert, responsive and attended Mass, the Vatican said, as the 88-year-old pontiff battles pneumonia and a complex lung infection.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: They crossed the Darien Gap to reach the US. Now, boat-by-boat, migrants are returning
item.description: PUERTO CARTÍ, Panama (AP) — They once braved the jungles of the Darien Gap, trekking days along the perilous migrant passage dividing Colombia and Panama with a simple goal: seek asylum in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATIAS DELACROIX and JUAN ZAMORANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Sudan’s military breaks paramilitary group's siege of crucial city
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Sudan’s military on Sunday broke a more than yearlong siege on the crucial city of Obeid, restoring access to a strategic area in the south-central region and strengthening crucial supply routes in its nearly two years of war against a notorious paramilitary group, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Masses of mourners attend the funeral of Hezbollah leader Nasrallah, 5 months after his killing
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Hundreds of thousands of people packed into a stadium in Beirut and nearby streets on Sunday for the funeral of Hezbollah's former leader, Hassan Nasrallah, nearly five months after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, ABBY SEWELL and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Conservative candidate pulls ahead in German election, according to early exit polls
item.description: Center-right opposition leader Friedrich Merz claimed victory in Germany’s national election on Sunday after exit polls showed his bloc in the lead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Surviving Hamas: The story of Keith Siegel, an Israeli American hostage
item.description: An Israeli-American released from Hamas captivity weeks ago is doing 'OK,' his wife tells NewsNation's "Morning in America with Hena Doba.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy says he'd be ready to give up presidency if it brought peace, NATO membership
item.description: “If to achieve peace, you really need me to give up my post, I’m ready,” Zelenskyy said in response to a journalist's question on whether he’d trade his office for peace. “I can trade it for NATO.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Pope Francis had peaceful night's rest at hospital following respiratory crisis, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis, 88, rested during a peaceful ninth night at Rome's Gemelli Hospital after a respiratory crisis and blood transfusions, the Vatican said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wounded, recovered and back to war. Ukrainian soldiers are returning to battle after amputation
item.description: DONETSK REGION, Ukraine (AP) — The Ukrainian intelligence soldier doesn’t know how long his clinical death lasted after an explosive detonated beneath him. All Andrii Rubliuk remembers is overwhelming cold, darkness and fear.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, EVGENIY MALOLETKA and VASILISA STEPANENKO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is in critical condition
item.description: The 88-year-old Francis, who has been hospitalized for a week with a complex lung infection, also received blood transfusions after tests showed a condition associated with anemia, the Vatican said in a late update.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Winfield, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is in critical condition after a long respiratory crisis, requiring oxygen at high flow
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical condition Saturday after he suffered a prolonged asthmatic respiratory crisis while being treated for pneumonia and a complex lung infection, the Vatican said. The 88-year-old pope, who remains conscious, received “high flows” of oxygen to help him breathe.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SILVIA STELLACCI, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump-Putin summit preparations are underway, Russia says
item.description: Preparations are underway for a face-to-face meeting between U.S. President Donald Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin, Russia's deputy foreign minister said Saturday. It marked a clear departure from Western efforts to isolate Moscow over its war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump envoy visits injured Ukrainian soldiers to deliver message, gets asked simple question
item.description: Just days before the three-year anniversary of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Trump’s envoy seeks to end the bloody conflict and secure compensation from Ukraine for U.S. military aid to the war-torn nation that is fighting for its survival.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Houthis target US fighter jet, drone with SAM missiles for first time
item.description: Houthi rebels fired air missile at United States military fighter jets. While the missile did not strike the jet, the attempted attack escalated the altercation between the two sides.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sununu urges Zelensky to 'be careful' not to anger Trump
item.description: Former New Hampshire Gov. Chris Sununu (R) advised Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky during a recent interview to “be careful” not to anger President Trump. His warning comes as the two leaders have gone tit-for-tat in recent days after U.S.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Hamas releases 6 more hostages, signals readiness for next phase of shaky ceasefire
item.description: Hamas on Saturday released six Israeli hostages in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, signaling readiness for the next phase of a shaky ceasefire. Avera Mengistu and Tal Shoham were handed to the Red Cross and eventually to Israel in the southern Gazan city of Rafah.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Syrian suspect in Berlin Holocaust Memorial stabbing wanted to kill Jews, investigators say
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — The suspect in a stabbing attack at Berlin's Holocaust Memorial that seriously injured a Spanish tourist is a Syrian refugee who apparently wanted to kill Jews, investigators said Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Economy, immigration, Elon Musk at center of German election; conservative candidate favored to win
item.description: Germans go to the polls Sunday. Chancellor Scholz's Social Democrats are likely to lose to the conservative CDU party, as the right-wing Alternative for Germany party is likely to make gains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brace for a 'political revolution' in Europe, UK's Nigel Farage says after Vance's speech in Munich
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of the Reform UK political party, told Fox News Digital he believes a "political revolution," like the one in the U.S., is headed for Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas releases more hostages in exchange for more than 600 Palestinian prisoners as part of ceasefire deal
item.description: Hamas freed more hostages on Saturday in exchange for more than 600 Palestinian prisoners as part of the delicate ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Delta faces first lawsuits days after plane flips in Toronto
item.description: All 21 people injured in the crash landing have been released from the hospital, and no one was killed.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Suspect arrested after stabbing at Berlin's Holocaust memorial near US embassy
item.description: German police arrested a suspect in a stabbing attack at Berlin's Holocaust memorial on Friday. No motive has been given for the attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Remains of Shiri Bibas, mom of two killed, allegedly returned to Israel following Hamas' broken promise
item.description: The remains of a woman killed while in captivity by Hamas was supposedly handed over to Israel on Friday, a day after her body was supposed to be brought back.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands rally in Slovakia to mark the 2018 slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee
item.description: BRATISLAVA, Slovakia (AP) — Thousands rallied all across Slovakia on Friday to mark the seventh anniversary of the slayings of an investigative journalist and his fiancee. The rallies are part of a wave of protests against the pro-Russia policies of populist Prime Minister Robert Fico.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An encroaching desert threatens to swallow Mauritania’s homes and history
item.description: CHINGUETTI, Mauritania (AP) — For centuries, poets, scholars and theologians have flocked to Chinguetti, a trans-Saharan trading post home to more than a dozen libraries containing thousands of manuscripts. But it now stands on the brink of oblivion.
item.dc:creator: SAM METZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis is 'fine,' condition not life-threatening, doctors say
item.description: Pope Francis "is fine" as his medical condition is not life-threatening, but he is not being released from the hospital yet, this doctors in Italy announced.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu calls for 'revenge' after Hamas returns wrong remains
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is vowing revenge after Hamas delivered the body of an anonymous woman instead of Shiri Bibas in violation of the current ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Singer of Canadian anthem at 4 Nations Face-Off changes lyric to protest Trump's 51st state remarks
item.description: The anthem singer who performed the Canadian anthem prior to the 4 Nations Face-Off championship game Thursday night changed a lyric in “O Canada” as a response to U.S. President Donald Trump's repeated remarks about making the country the 51st state.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN WHYNO and KEN POWTAK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel identifies remains of child hostages but says another body from Hamas was not their mother
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — The Israeli military said Friday it had positively identified the remains of two young hostages but another body released by Hamas under a ceasefire deal was not the boys’ mother as the militant group had promised.
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN, JOSEF FEDERMAN AND WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Medical oxygen in short supply in many parts of world: Research
item.description: "We found that more than 5 billion people—ie, more than 60% of the world's population—do not have access to safe, quality, and affordable medical oxygen services," the commission wrote.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's envoy arrives in Kyiv as US policy shifts toward Russia
item.description: Keith Kellogg, the U.S. special envoy for Ukraine and Russia, arrived in Kyiv on Wednesday for talks with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and military commanders, as the U.S. shifted its policy away from years of efforts to isolate Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN SPIKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hundreds of FAA workers laid off amid staffing shortages, raising safety concerns
item.description: Hundreds of probationary workers with the Federal Aviation Administration were laid off over the weekend, raising concerns about air traffic safety as the agency struggles to meet hiring goals amid increased air traffic.
item.dc:creator: Ben Bradley, WGN 9
